project_overview.project_name: Strengthening Honduras' National Civil Registry and Identification Ecosystem Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To strengthen Honduras´s national civil registry and ID ecosystem by improving the birth registration process and identification of minors and enabling access of all individuals to public and private services.
project_overview.beneficiaries: All individuals residing in Honduras and Honduran citizens abroad; specifically targeting children aged 6-17 years, newborns (0-6 months), women and girls in rural areas, indigenous peoples, Afro-Hondurans, youth at risk of migrating, returnees, people with disabilities, and government/private sector institutions requiring ID verification services

primary_locations: Rural areas with emphasis on remote and vulnerable communities
Urban centers including Tegucigalpa and San Pedro Sula
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Honduras faces significant violence and gang activity (36 murders per 100,000 in 2022), high migration pressures, and climate vulnerability. Project targets vulnerable populations including youth at risk of migrating, internally displaced persons, and those vulnerable to climate impacts. Not classified as fragile state but has substantial governance and security challenges.

key_components.component_name: Improvement of civil registration and minors' identification coverage
key_components.description: Strengthens civil registry and identification systems through: (1) Birth registration modernization and automation for live births 0-6 months including platform design, equipment, capacity building, and procedures for vulnerable populations; (2) Expansion of identification system coverage for ages 6-17 through enrollment strategy, mobile teams, capacity building, communications, and ID card issuance with emphasis on rural girls and vulnerable groups.

key_components.component_name: Modernizing civil registration and digital identification services
key_components.description: Develops digital ID services through: (1) Strengthening National Identification System (SIN) interoperability for ID verification and authentication services to public and private sectors, and certification model for digital signatures; (2) Developing omnichannel service model including SIN interoperability improvements, website strengthening, grievance mechanism, accessible communications (Braille, indigenous languages, sign language), and auxiliary service centers and self-service kiosks.

key_components.component_name: Strengthening the institutional capacity of the National Registry of Persons
key_components.description: Supports RNP capacity building through: (1) Regulatory and operational framework strengthening including legal review, internal manuals, governance framework, and Privacy Impact Assessments; (2) Promotion of inclusive registration culture through outreach campaigns and change management strategy; (3) Cyber resilience and digital infrastructure enhancement including technological infrastructure resilience, data protection protocols, Security Operations Center design, and environmental management standards; (4) Capacity building and project management including planning, financial, fiduciary, M&E, and E&S risk management capabilities.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: National Registry of Persons (RNP) with Project Implementation Unit (PIU) comprising: project coordinator, financial management specialist, procurement specialist, monitoring and evaluation specialist, and environmental and social specialist. UN Agency (likely UNDP) engaged as co-implementing agent for fiduciary, procurement, and specific activity implementation.
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Close coordination with Health Ministry (for birth registration in hospitals and midwife networks), Education Ministry (for school-based enrollment of minors), municipalities, indigenous peoples associations, civil society organizations, and private sector institutions. Subsidiary Agreement between Government and RNP. Service Level Agreements with partner institutions.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (September 2023). UN Agency handles majority of procurement as co-implementing agent. RNP PIU performs low-risk processes. Procurement Plan covering first 18 months. Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) with market analysis. Prior review thresholds observed. Standard procurement documents used. Cybersecurity guidance mandatory for IT procurements. Systematic Tracking of Exchanges in Procurement (STEP) system used.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: M&E specialist at RNP monitors progress toward results indicators. Semi-annual reports submitted to World Bank including implementation progress, outputs delivered, execution estimates, and disbursement projections. Monitoring framework includes gender-disaggregated indicators. Evaluations planned to deepen understanding of gaps and inform corrective actions. User satisfaction measured. Service satisfaction surveys conducted.

identified_risks.risk_description: Elections of new RNP commissioners scheduled for September 2024 could result in changes to government priorities affecting project implementation. Substantial commitment from current authorities may not continue.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Work with both senior and technical level staff at RNP and Ministry of Finance (SEFIN) to ensure ownership and sustainability during transition period. Alignment with RNP Institutional Strategic Plan 2020-2024 and Government Plan.

identified_risks.risk_description: Project comprises technically complex activities involving sophisticated ICT technologies. Substantial risks related to biometric data collection and data protection, especially for school-aged children. Data collection, storage and transmission must be secure and trusted across ecosystem.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Finance enhancements to enabling legal and regulatory framework. Engage technical experts to advise RNP on data protection measures. Implement data Privacy Impact Assessments (PIA) to address potential impacts of biometric data collection and processing. Strengthen cybersecurity capabilities. Design Security Operations Center.

identified_risks.risk_description: RNP has limited technical and project management capacity. Roll-out of enrollment and registry programs requires strengthening capacity of partner institutions staff at Health and Education Ministries. Implementation requires dynamic flow of complex procurement processes.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive training program for enrollment staff on effective, friendly, and gender-sensitive processes with protocols to overcome inclusion barriers. Dedicated PIU team with UN Agency as agent for fiduciary and procurement support. Close FM supervision every six months in first year. External audit firm hired with acceptable ToR.

identified_risks.risk_description: Project envisions enrollment of school-aged children and may face opposition from civil society groups with adverse impact on implementation. Limited capacity to reach certain stakeholders including indigenous peoples, rural households, female heads of households, and migrants.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strengthen RNP capacity to effectively coordinate and liaise with stakeholders. Implement change management strategy to facilitate implementation and overcome institutional resistance. Comprehensive outreach and communication campaigns. Stakeholder consultation plan with adaptations for indigenous peoples. Omnichannel service model adapted to vulnerable groups' requirements.

identified_risks.risk_description: RNP lacks previous experience implementing World Bank-financed projects. Need to create PIU and train personnel on FM and disbursement procedures. Overall FM risk assessed as substantial.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Co-implementing UN Agency hired for implementation. RNP hires qualified FM staff experienced in World Bank projects. FM personnel undergo specific training on FM and disbursement procedures. Close FM supervision every six months during first year. Project Operations Manual details processes and responsibilities. External audit firm hired following acceptable ToR and International Standards on Auditing (ISAs).

identified_risks.risk_description: Honduras faces persistent inflation, slower growth, and poverty challenges. Unemployment above pre-pandemic levels, particularly for women. These conditions could affect project sustainability and beneficiary engagement.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project design includes efficiency gains and cost savings that contribute to public spending efficiency. Economic analysis shows positive IRR (24.4%) and NPV ($37.7 million) under baseline scenario and stress tests. Project expected to have negligible impact on operating costs once implementation complete.

identified_risks.risk_description: Key E&S risks include: waste generation from ID printing/plastification and electronic waste; occupational health and safety hazards; limited capacity to reach vulnerable stakeholders in remote areas; potential SEA/SH risks from mobile brigades engaging with school-aged children; data protection risks for biometric collection of minors.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Commitment Plan (ESCP) prepared and disclosed. Dedicated full-time E&S specialist in PIU. Waste management requirements included. OSH requirements specified. SEA/SH protocol for security forces and enrollment agents in POM. Privacy Impact Assessments conducted. Grievance mechanism established. Consultation plan with adaptations for indigenous peoples. Biannual Bank implementation support missions to monitor E&S performance.

identified_risks.risk_description: RNP has very limited procurement capacity and no previous experience with World Bank-funded projects. Project includes large and complex procurement activities requiring innovative technical specifications. Risk that RNP not sufficiently involved in decision-making and lacks access to procurement records.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: UN Agency hired to handle all procurement processes and contract management. UNDP selected given previous experience with Identifícate project and knowledge of market/supply chains. Small PIU created with Procurement Specialist to follow up and maintain records. World Bank provides support developing technical specifications and ToR. Targeted market analysis performed. Post-review missions annually with 20% contract sample.

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project includes: (1) Project-level grievance mechanism to address queries or complaints regarding identity data, error correction, and RNP services; (2) Separate grievance mechanism for project workers; (3) World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available for project-affected communities; (4) World Bank Accountability Mechanism (Inspection Panel and Dispute Resolution Service) available. Target of 70% of queries/complaints satisfactorily resolved by end of project.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project specifically targets girls in rural areas (90% of rural girls 6-17 to receive ID cards by project end). Recognizes gender disparities in rural registration, higher adolescent motherhood rates in rural areas (28.3% vs 16.5% urban), and barriers women face in exercising identity rights. Gender-sensitive enrollment processes planned. Capacity building for staff on gender-sensitive service delivery. Communications campaigns designed avoiding gender stereotypes. Extended business hours considered to meet women's needs. Women included in registry teams. Child-friendly waiting areas. Monitoring framework includes gender-disaggregated indicators. Indexing newborn/child data with parental information enables women to assert associated rights.
vulnerable_groups: Children aged 0-17 years (especially minors without ID)
Indigenous peoples (Misquitos, Lenca, and other communities)
People with disabilities (target of 22,000 children with disabilities to receive ID cards)
Children vulnerable to trafficking and gang recruitment
Persons in circumstances requiring access to social services during climate disasters

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project addresses conflict-related vulnerabilities through: (1) Identification of minors to reduce vulnerability to gang recruitment and forced displacement; (2) Support for migrant children and returnees lacking ID documents; (3) Timely birth registration to facilitate access to social protection and reduce trafficking risk; (4) Enrollment strategy emphasizing youth at risk of migrating; (5) Support for internally displaced persons through March 2023 Law ensuring RNP provides identification to those whose documents lost/damaged due to forced displacement; (6) Procedures and manuals to safeguard inclusion of vulnerable populations; (7) Capacity building and communications targeting vulnerable groups; (8) Grievance mechanisms to address concerns of affected populations.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Honduras faces multiple fragility factors: (1) High violence and homicide rates (36 per 100,000 in 2022, one of region's highest); (2) Persistent gang activity and forced recruitment of youth; (3) High migration pressures with unaccompanied minors in migrant caravans; (4) Poverty (52.4% in 2022) and extreme poverty (13.3%); (5) High inequality (Gini 47.5%); (6) Weak institutional capacity and governance challenges; (7) Limited human capital development; (8) Significant climate vulnerability to hurricanes, floods, droughts; (9) Weak civil registry and identification systems enabling exclusion and vulnerability. Project directly addresses identification gaps that increase vulnerability to trafficking, gang recruitment, and exclusion from services.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security considerations include: (1) Mobile brigades accompanied by security personnel during enrollment activities; (2) Protocol for security forces behavior when engaging with stakeholders, especially school-aged children, to mitigate SEA/SH risks; (3) Cybersecurity enhancements to protect sensitive biometric and personal data; (4) Design of Security Operations Center to monitor, detect, investigate, and respond to data breaches and cyber threats; (5) Data protection and privacy protocols to safeguard personal data; (6) Resilience of digital infrastructure to climate-induced shocks and natural disasters; (7) Business continuity planning and disaster recovery protocols for critical ID services.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Project specifically addresses displacement and refugee populations: (1) March 2023 Law ensures RNP provides identification to individuals whose documents lost/damaged due to forced displacement; (2) Project supports identification of internally displaced persons to access relief programs and government services; (3) Enrollment strategy targets migrant children and returnees; (4) Over 2,300 returned migrant children and adolescents assisted by DINAF in 2023 (30% traveled alone); (5) Project aims to reduce vulnerability of unaccompanied minors through identification; (6) Timely birth registration and ID cards facilitate access to post-disaster relief and emergency services; (7) Digital authentication services enable quick rollout of disaster and emergency relief programs by validating identity and eligibility of beneficiaries; (8) Project supports climate-resilient identification systems to enable government response during natural disasters and emergencies.
